reviews.review_text:
It's worth it. Jump in and get beaten to death by starving bandits. Jump back in to get [i] nearly [/i] beaten to death by bandits and become stronger. Become so strong you can 1v50 those bandits with your bare hands while naked and carrying the unconscious body of their leader then Naruto run that boy to the nearest city for a bounty.

You can play this game in so many different ways that I keep coming back to try out a new idea for a playthrough. All of that is [b] before [/b] you dip your toes into the insanely dedicated modding community for this game over on the steam workshop.

Is it janky? [b] OH MY YES [/b]

Do you sometimes have to reload because your squad member glitched into a wall and you can't get him out? [b] More frequently than you should, yes. [/b]

Is it worth all the jank? [b] Without any doubt, yes. [/b]

Kenshi is a gem of a game and I couldn't be more excited to see what's in store for Kenshi II.

Many Ukrainians play your game, and we love it. The game doesn’t support our language. We understand that, until recently, not everyone in Ukraine needed or used Russian, but now we need a Ukrainian localization—if not in Kenshi, then in Kenshi 2.
Please add Ukrainian.

Oh, I never wrote a review. Well this game deserves one, atleast a small one.

Simply one of the best game experiences I have ever had. The screenshots and videos don't do it justice, it was like an odd art style that I enjoyed, when you play the game it's a whole other experience. I truly love this game, I love seeing all the unknown lands, feeling real fear and wearyness, feeling real pride when I am cabable of acomplishing something, because I know how hard I fought to get there. weeks and months of toil, and starving, all to see the world and start my own empire in the end. A truly wonderful game.

I sincerely congratulate Lo-Fi Games, and especially Chris Hunt, on reaching three million copies sold of Kenshi. It is a well-deserved result of a long journey-everything that was done was not in vain.
Kenshi is one of those rare games that truly gives a sense of freedom, adventure, and your own unique story. Thank you for the incredible world, atmosphere, and hundreds of hours of unforgettable experiences. Wishing you continued success with the development of the sequel!

Being allied with things that skin people alive is less controversial than being against slavery
What a game

Ah, Kenshi.  I purchased this game after watching numerous Youtube videos of people doing their own creative / fun "runs" (such as a slave to business owner run, or a farming run, etc).  Each looked pretty neat, and as an avid Rimworld fan I thought "Hey, this is sort of like Rimworld but in third person 3d!".  Next steam sale, I excitedly hit purchase.

Well, I stand here now having installed the game and tried it for myself, and... "underwhelmed" doesn't do it justice.  Have you ever played really old MMO's, like EverQuest or DAOC?  Imagine hopping back in now, same graphics and all, and there was no tutorial or training missions to at least familiarize yourself with the controls.  Great, I did it to myself again, I purchased yet another game where I'm expected to watch hours of Youtube videos for stuff that the game itself refuses to teach.

There seems like there's potential here, if you want a super sandboxy open world type thing where there isn't any sort of story, plot, or world events.  It does seem to be one of those "Everything and the kitchen sink, too" sort of games, where if you have an idea - you can accomplish it.  I'm just typically not that creative, and need at least SOME degree of direction  to start playing a game. 

I may revise my review in the future if I give it another try, but I fear that this may be one of those games that's going to be more interesting to watch other people play, than to actually play myself.  Maybe I'm missing something, but my brain was practically SCREAMING at me, "Now what?".

**Update with more detail**
Gave it one more quick shot since I'm a bit of a masochist / hate wasting money on something I just purchased.  I genuinely feel like I'm missing something crucial to having fun here, because both times I got dropped into the "Wanderer" start - I felt like a toddler who was abandoned in the middle of an airport; confused, lost, directionless, and perhaps a bit scared.  After wandering aimlessly around the starting area for a while, I finally had enough and uninstalled this thing.  Main issues were:

**UI / Graphics**
I am not usually against "deep, impenetrable" UI's - hell, one of my favorite games is the X-series of space games, which is perhaps the most menu driven game I've ever played.  Likewise, graphics aren't usually all that important to me, with Rimworld being my favorite game.  Despite this somehow though, Kenshi's UI is downright horrid and clunky, with buttons scattered all over the screen that go completely unexplained.  The graphics itself don't do it any justice either, with animations and models looking like something out of a circa ~2000 game (Deus Ex 1, etc).

The game very much could do with a UI overhaul, and perhaps a graphics overhaul mod as well.  Do these exist?

**Gameplay / Plot**
Biggest issue right here  - having watched folks online play this game, the thought never occurred to me that this would be a total grind-fest in order to accomplish anything.  Skills take hours and hours to level up to a level that would be considered usable, your character is utterly useless at the beginning and will fold like a cheap piece of origami.  Folks love to say that this is the point, and you "need to embrace losing" - but to be frank, that isn't really fun.  It's tedious.  I hate the whole "Losing is Fun" mentality from the Dwarf Fortress fandom, it's no different here.

Add in the fact that there's no quests, no direction, not even a vague sense of plot to really dig into.  The question I keep asking myself is "Why am I doing this?  What is the point?".  If I free all the slaves (where are they, how do I find them, who knows!), it doesn't seem to make any difference to the world, and doesn't net you any rewards.  Likewise if I make a outpost in the middle of the desert, it just feels so... aimless.  Like being the only person playing an MMO that everyone else stopped playing 10 years ago.  Perhaps there's mods out there that let me start as a "pre-built" character with combat abilities already existing, but it still begs the question of 'for what purpose?".

**Over-reliance on external material / time investment*
Posts often refer people to watch various "newbie" youtube videos, or read guides in order to properly start this game without feeling totally lost.  Some may like this, but this is a telltale sign of poor game design if you ask me; I shouldn't have to quit the game to watch an hour+ of someone explaining basic mechanics - that's what tutorials and newbie zones are for.  Please don't force me to do 'homework' just because the devs are too lazy to put in tutorials (and yes, for a 1.0 release, it's pretty damn unexcuseable).

Likewise I frequently hear "Oh, you only played 3 hours, you really need to play at least 20 to truly have it click", and again - that doesn't exactly sound like good game design.  This sounds more like Stockholm Syndrome for video games, if something isn't enjoyable for the first hour, I'm not going to waste my time.  I also loathe the fact that so many people compared this game to Rimworld, since it's pretty much NOTHING alike.  I'd say it's closer to a Project Zomboid, but with a heck of a lot more bugs and a truly atrocious UI.

**Conclusion**
Kenshi checks a lot of the boxes I love in survival / third person games - a supposedly decent crafting system, the ability to colonize anywhere, build bases, etc.  That said, this is exactly why it's so disappointing that I found it so devoid of life, bland, an absolute chore to play, and a complete grindfest.  Maybe some folks can suggest mods that fix a lot of my gripes, maybe they're "unfixable" and the problem is that I simply lack the ability to play "100% sandbox" games.  

All in all, I don't think this is for me.  This feels like an early access game that had development abandoned halfway through, they slapped a 1.0 on it and called it done.

I don't know, for a game that's described as this vast and complex sandbox where you can do anything, there sure is little incentive to do anything at all. In the standard start, you are plopped into a dead-ish little village where you basically can't afford anything other than food and bed. 
   I was really looking forward to exploring around, seeing if I can find a person to help or someone to follow, a cause to join, but apparently that's not a part of the game. There are mercenaries who want you to hire them, but there are no opportunities for yourself to offer your services to anyone else. In a game that bases its marketing on you being equal to the npcs and not a "chosen one" character, that's extremely contradictory. You are the chosen one, because you're the only character that seems to act on their own. I found a faction that called themselves the Shinobi Thieves and thought they would be my saving, that they would give me a story to create, a cause to join. But alas, all you get is "So you want to become a Shinobi Thief hehe?" You can't even ask what the hell that means. It doesn't matter that "all NPCs have their own lives" if you can't even talk to the majority of them. 
   It's supposedly a good and huge sandbox that gives you loads of tools to create your own narrative, but honestly the seams of this simulation are so easy to ripple and see through that I felt I was better off refunding. I know I played a very short time, but after fully exploring the starting location, there was nothing to engage with. If I were to "create my own narrative", it would all be in my head, not in the game.
The grind is good, but its masochistic. Shoddy game design. Not a smooth game.
I gotta tell you, it was perfect. Perfect. Everything, down to the last minute details

It is an interesting take of a old western japanese steam punk medieval fantasy sword fantasy kung fu post apocalyptic sci fi setting. I've never seen someone mash this many ideas and just get a setting that feels cohesive and immersive. So the world building is good, now the gameplay, that needs work. Fortunately, this is one of the most moddable games out there, reducing raid frequencies, increasing NPCs, improving combat, rebalancing the economy... And now with Re Kenshi it is getting to another level again.

i have bought Kenshi three times, Or more specifically, bought and refunded twice, 

At first i was young, wasnt sure what this Fu**Ass game was, why was i so bad? who made this ugly ♥♥♥♥.

Second i was slightly older, thought, "maybe i was wrong" played it for 30 minutes, genuinely couldnt get into it, and refunded. 

now the third time, now i get it. Maybe it was the few hundred hours of rimworld, and a handful of hours being castrated by StoneShard that made me think, Maybe i was too harsh. 

40+ hours later and i think i have fell in love. This game is not perfect, and it absolutely does not hold your hand, but if you get your footing, you can begin to experience what an amazing game this truly is. 

Never have i been more happy to spend a ridiculously low amount of money for a game i see myself playing into the hundreds of hours.
Not my type of game - world just feels kind of empty and fighting is a bit of a chore. If you can push through that I could see it opening up a lot

I always struggle to review games like this, because the community is amazing and consistently releases new content and mods for this game. With that, I'll just be touching on the vanilla release, as with mods you can make it into whatever you want.

So Kenshi, what is it? It's a squad based open world game with a focus on community, allowing you to build your own structures, towns, and even empire. Unlike most other games of this nature, theres a huge focus on individual's rather than hero units or trainable soldiers. Your main team will be your main team for a majority of your sessions. I found it quite difficult to manage more than 3 squads at a time, 2 exploration teams and a 3rd to run and guard the base. This works well and allows you to limit the size of your operation to whatever you personally find managable.

Kenshi is a real time strategy, so you wont be influencing fights directly as much as youll be managing squad macro and keeping your people alive and healthy. The main hurdle when starting is learning that you're MEANT to get your ass kicked a few times. It's just counterintuitive and hard to learn. 

I have nothing but nice things to say about this janky buggy mess, I adore it and if you put the time in you might as well. New players would probably benifet from a tutorial start, but I'm not sure Kenshi even cares if you play it. Its a desolate world that would keep on scrapping by, with or without you. The ancient robots, ruins and sparse wastes hold several worlds worth of lore and story just waiting to be found. The lore is probably the best part of Kenshi, realizing this world is much older and much more complicated than you ever imagined. Strong 8/10 from me.

Idiosyncratically tedious and abusive.  I like the concepts and ideas, and some of the things it does are a novelty, but the almost two dozen hours I put in to the game gave me little to go on.  You can grind stats in bizarre ways; toughness for characters equates to intentionally being beaten into unconsciousness.  You can build an outpost, and the moment you've established an empty hovel with nothing of worth you'll be forced to roll out the welcome mat to punitive events that teleport to your door step.  There's no story and characters are few and far between, with little dialogue when you can even find it.  They're less 'homunculus' and more affectations of a barren sandbox.

Ultimately, I think this isn't really much of a video game, and instead is more akin to a bucket of broken erector sets.  For a certain kind of person, they'll make their own fun with that and get hundreds of hours out of it.  I'm not one of them, unfortunately.

I'm the sort of person that should love an open game like this. I loved Morrowind, I loved Warband, and I've played games just like this on Kongregate back in the day (more recommendations are very welcome!). But wow... this just completely misses for me.

There's almost entirely no sound at all. No music, no sfx. You just right click areas to do stuff, rather than feeling like you're actually playing a game. There's no quests, and no story. 

So far, I walk over to the iron, mine the iron, walk to town to sell, buy some stuff for my outpost, walk to outpost, wait for build, then walk back to iron, repeat. That loop takes like 15 minutes, and considering you just right click on the map to move, almost the ENTIRE 15 minutes is literally just me staring at the screen do it's thing, with zero input. Realistically, this is almost an idler game. However, if you do in fact idle and go AFK, some random little thing will attack you, and since you don't give inputs (or it's too strong) you just lose. Losing "can" be as simple as being injured and needing to hobble back to town, or, as in my one case, you're unconscious, with no food or medicine, so you can't do anything at all to help yourself, and you just die. Gameover, because it's permadeath. So you're forced to sit there watching the painfully long walk, the extremely painfully long mining process, etc. I'm sure there's good elements to this game, it looks super promising, but WOW it's boring to actually sit through, and you MUST sit through it, or else keep making backup saves in case it goes bad.

I'm still really wanting to get into it, because I can see there's a gem here, but it's a diamond in the rough... a lot of rough... and I just don't want to subject myself to painfully boring torturously bad gameplay, just for that little nugget of goodness.

I really want to get lost in Kenshi. It has all the makings of a game you feel completely immersed in -- minus the visuals obviously. The world feels so unique and free. 

However, the camera and the point and click controls are just so cumbersome. It removes me completely from the experience. If this had a standard anchored third person POV and normal WASD player movement controls, I would play this game for days at a time. I think this game would be hugely popular if the camera/controls weren't so abnormal. Until then, it's just sort of infuriating.

Praying Kenshi 2 has better control options.

I spent 170 hours in this game building my own self-sustaining city, training every recruit and getting them equipment, as well as allying every single faction in the game by spending legally acquired 25+ MILLION cats on buying out hurdreds of slaves. And I didn't regret a second of it.
Even with extreme amount of grinding I had in the process (and to clarify, I am not a fan of grinding), I had a lot of fun.
This game is filled with so many small interactions that you never know what else this game has in it. I was pleasantly surprised when I came to Spring to sell some stuff (previously having a rough encounter with an elder beak thing), and then one of the anti-slavers gave me a medkit and followed me for a few hours to ensure that I would be okay.
Kenshi feels like an ancient artifact: you know just how much it contains, yet never it's full potential.
Sure, I expirienced a bunch of bugs and crashes along the way (so a friendly reminder to save regularly), but I never had something significantly game-breaking (though cases with turret guards falling down and getting stuck in a wall are annoying).
This game is one of a kind, the sandbox that you will enjoy playing one way or another.

Oh, and did i mention that this game has mods? In fact, I am yet to expirience them myself!
So yes, this game is 100% worth it's price. I am glad that I have it, and I recommend everyone to try it out.

For $8 this game is a ton of fun. Scratches an itch deep down... gives Rimworld and Diablo vibes in a way (disclaimer: not a diablo gamer). Mods are great, game is great. 

For those worried about the length of gameplay and grindy nature - fear not, there are plenty of game speed tools to better manage your time.

It's okay if you are okay with games don't really leading you to something.
You have to get the "use" out of it yourself, or the way to get fun out of it.
It's not something for everyone.

Overall amazing game and friend told me about a year ago and he loves the game! I love the game thanks to the community behind it but I just wish that there was small updates to the path finder, when mining I "apparently" assaulted someone out of no where and wish there animals that we could use to ride across the map. I got so fed up when I first played it but if I just stuck with it..... I would have kept playing it when I first bought it. I am glad that I tried it again!

*be a skeleton
*lead bandits to your nearby city to get the guards to kill them
*take their stuff and sell it
*build a house with the money you got
*realize you took damage and there's no skeleton repair kits or anything nearby
*go on a journey to a city that has these things
*get ripped apart
*rage quit
peak game.

i hate point and click games but Kenshi change that i only have 23 hours in so far and i went through so much already and im already hooked on this game cant wait to try a playthrough with some mods.My 23 hours of gameplay so far- i started at the hub as a human found some food off a dead animal picked up some loot off of a dead bandit after watching him and his buddy get jumped by nomads and his herd went to go mine for a bit to make some quick cats after that i went to shrek kingdom well i tried 20 hungry bandits chased me back to the hub gates a few seconds after leaving thank the stars for the hub guards they killed them and healed me, after waking up i tried again and we made it. I bought/earned the trust of a few Shrek servants that wished to be warriors few saw a challenge to try and keep me alive (helps help buddy) went back to the hub and bought a bull from those nomads also picked up a human cook and hunter from the bar, with my party now 9 plus a bull strong we went to attack any group of hungry bandits we took down a few we then started to make a outpost by the hub until some holy nation preacher came and gave me their book....i threw it on the ground in the sand about five days later 25ish men from the holy nation showed up on our front doorstep right where their book still laid in the sand at their feet, they where  ready for war, we managed to kill two that strayed away from their pack but after carefully thinking we decided to pick up our fallen and... RUN AWAY!!  Now we build our strength and our numbers and wage war on the holy nation. Yeah this game is pretty fun for me.
Bought this game a few days ago and can't stop playing! Highly recommend.

There is but one light on this brutally beautiful moon and that light is Beep. Without Beep the game feels almost empty. Beep is the shining light that draws me back along with many other things. But mostly Beep. 10/10 game cant wait for kenshi 2 and hopefully more Beep.
After 1k hours and finally getting past the noob stage I think it's fair to review. It's a pretty good game.

Kenshi is Kenshi, there's no other game quite like it.

Persevere, stay determined, the game does not stop at failure, the only STOP button is death.

This is amazing. I actually feel bad buying this on 72% sale O.o 

I was unsure at first with the looks, complex seeming menus.
BUT
after learning the basics and getting eaten by cannibals in the first week, I've been unable to put this game down.

Currently on day 40 with my second run, feeling like I'm just scratching the surface. Excited to see how big I can go and what splendid adventures await me.

I'm aware that this won't be for everyone, it's quite challenging. If you enjoy being punished like me, give it a try!
ah yes the game where the world is my oyster the only problem is that oyster is horribly mutated and hates you and everyone you love and pays mercenaries to randomly come and beat you up 10/10

Decided to jump in for my first game before bed. Had no idea what to do. Joining the thieves guild cost money. Went inside to use their facilities to train and to steal from. Got caught stealing or accidently hit someone training. Was beat unconscious and kicked out to die in the desert.

10/10 Experience. Would definitely recommend.

This is not just a game, its altogether a new experience. 
It has very interesting layers of complexity, while still being fun and not grind-y. 

What I find mind-blowing its that you can really play it in dozens of deifferent ways, and its trully a sandbox game in that sense. Its more like a world engine which you are but a small part of it. How did the developer (Chris/Lo-Fi) acomplished this while staying afar from the traditional "boxes" that games usually confine you in.

And it has mods for about everything as well!

According to steam I only have 13 hours. This is because a lot of my playtime has been offline. It'd probably be way closer to 60 if it had all my offline hours.
Kenshi is a game that you either will absolutely love or ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ despise. It is probably the most open RPG to ever exist. There aren't really quests, there's no real storyline. It is all up to you to decide what your goals and story will be. It is also a game that is tough as ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ nails. If you are someone that hates having to restart over and over, hates losing lots of progress due to your mistakes, etc. you will hate this game. I however, do not. I love when a game beats me down to my knees, so long as I know it is something that could still fairly be beaten if I simply learned how.

There is a very steep learning curve and I'm still figuring it out with systems I haven't even touched yet, but I can say this game has an insane amount of depth to it along with some jank. The leveling system is unique in that you have skill levels, not character levels that increase according to where those stats are relative to whatever you are fighting/doing. The cool part is that you can manipulate this with various temporary stat buffs/debuffs. So it becomes a balancing act of how can I game the system for maximum stat gains without dying or being too inefficient. 

There are plenty of things that might seem like a negative or bad, like losing a limb or getting knocked out, enslaved, shotting squamates, etc, but you can game that for gain as well. 

Learning how things work and progression feels really rewarding, worth a try if you are on the fence.

I got tired of being a slave to the capitalistic, nepotist and elitist western civilisation so I decided to hop on kenshi to be a slave in game to a capitalistic, nepotist and elitist fictional civilisation instead

that said, I 100% recommend this game. determination and imagination are going to be your best weapons to survive and get into the game. Once you got that you're in for a few hundred hours.

big thanks to the modders of the game as well, they're some of the most passionate i've seen

Kenshi is the most hit or miss game out there, and there isn't really anything like it out there that comes close to Kenshi in quality. But it hits good. Mod support is massive and works nice with existing saves as well, realistically if you want a mod someone's made something like it, and learning to mod isn't too difficult.

"Slavery is useful because level up your stats"
One of the Greatest open world survival games of all time right here! Super excited for Kenshi 2!!!
My favorite computer game of all time, competing with some later age classics like diablo 2 age of destruction, age of empires 2, world of warcraft when it first came out, and half life.
I have 300+ hours and I have yet to exact my revenge on everybody I plan to punish for various reasons.

I first create a one-punch lady who has a heavy weapon wielding shek mommy sidekick Ruka. We conquer the entirety of holy nation with out dear crossbowman buddy Green. Our days were going pretty pristine until we met the [b]the biggest opponent[/b] so far...

[b]USER ERROR.[/b]

[i]I create a new save and overwrite my 150HR  save mentioned above. [/i]

I go cry about 15 minutes, but I remember I'm playing Kenshi so I ready myself for another +100HR CBT session.

I love this game. letting yourself get punished creates amazing stories.

Kenshi is the only game where being completely demolished qualifies as character growth.
You begin as weak as a wet paper towel, get mugged by homeless people trying to survive, captured by slavers, and gobbled up alive by fauna that could have come from a bad dream, yet still you wind up feeling:
“Yeah… one more hour.”
The absolute highlight is the tutorial.
In most games, the tutorial teaches you how to play.
In Kenshi, it’s literally just “good luck buddy,” throwing you into the desert without clothes.
10/10, best stockholm syndrome simulation.

you start as nobody, get beaten up by goats, lose a limb, you get better, get beaten up by goats again and lose another limb. Terrible. 10/10.

P.S: Play it while listening to Pink Floyd

The game is truly amazing, and the modding community is equally as amazing. I wish I had been aware of this gem disguised as a game earlier. 11/10

Don't be fooled by the graphics, as Mods and the gameplay will make you forget there was even a problem.

 I will write a more detailed reveiw after finally making a perfect mod collection.

Um dos melhores jogos que eu já joguei na minha vida. Um RPG hardcore que vai te espancar até você conseguir revidar. Os sistemas de gameplay são únicos e divertidos, todo lugar que você vai tem um personagem único com história pra contar, com diálogos únicos que contam a história do mundo pós pós pós apocalíptico ou que apenas servem como conversa entre personagens - que muitas vezes é chega a ser bem cômica. Por mais que o mundo desse jogo seja extremamente macabro e violento, as pessoas que ali vivem não deixam de ter um senso de humor. 
A direção de arte é magnifica, com vários inimigos interessantes e diversos, desde seres humanos até pessoas inseto e robôs. 
A soundtrack do jogo é emocionante. Dá uma sensação incrível quando você está passando pelo deserto e algum track começa a tocar. O sentimento de descoberta, de estar em uma jornada - é muito bonito.

Recomendo MUITO esse jogo, por mais que seja dificil de entender no começo justamente por ser tão diferente.

Ran half way across the map to find some shoes. Punched a guy to death. Was beat to death by that guys friends.

Never found shoes. 


ITS PEAK

While I do understand the potential appeal of this game, the game is seriously unforgiving. 
With the poor graphics, UI and lack of feedback, it just makes it a chore to play.

I have watched my partner play Kenshi for many years and always enjoyed watching her play it, and never thought I would as I prefer CO-OP or multiplayer games. 
When the sale came up recently I thought it was about time I give it a try.
Simply put I AM ADDICTED! 
The options you have playing this game *
From starting off as a slave with no limbs and grinding your way out and getting limbs again,
to starting out in the holy nation with some money and security and grinding to make a whole village for your crew! 
*The mods are endless!
huge props go to the community of this game always trying to add the best quality of life mods and the straight up ridiculous game breaking mods that may be fun to use in the future.
*The excitement I have waiting for Kenshi 2!
to one day come out HOPING that it is CO-OP as I would love to play with my partner!

I know I do not have a lot of hours on this game but I have watched my partner play it for years now. The only downside I can see so far is when I am not on Kenshi I find myself daydreaming about what my next plan is for my crew in Kenshi. Or if I have enough time in the day to add a slave character save game and play multiple saves in one day xD 
I feel an itch that I need to scratch with this game and I feel myself getting pulled into it every day. 

Amazing game! Much love! 

Kenny.

13 hours into the game, I can see WHY the fan base said it's one game that kept them playing for years. At first expecting this harsh world to be stressful, but it's surprisingly... NOT as stressful as I thought it would be due to it's slow progression, and time pacing at various play speed for the player, you almost don't want the time to pass too fast or a random slave trader just jump you and kill your crew. 

I am glad I joined the Kenshi band, and happy to find a world where I can bring my own characters and spend thousands of hours in and not be bored, this looks like that world. Bravo on the game design, the in depth world building, truly wonderful work of world building.

Kenshi might be one of the weirdest games I have. This is a compliment, it’s like a RPG simulator that takes place on an alien world.

It’s unforgiving, sometimes incredibly frustrating. Still - one of my top 10 games of all time.


Beep beep.

Kenshi is a brilliant game. The game facilitates and actively encourages you to create your own story-and you'll never forget these stories.

Here's one of mine:

Enter Scratch the Greenlander. He's nothing but a worthless wanderer trying to make a name for himself.  For a long time he struggled to make a living and he feels purposeless. He was totally unremarkable and the major factions of Kenshi had no idea he even existed. 

One day he decided that enough was enough and he would not go hungry any longer. And so,.Scratch started his journey towards becoming a well known thief.

For days on end he worked his trade, leaving people waking up with fewer possessions than they had yesterday. Not being too reckless, Scratch trained "The Way of the Blade" in case things went south. 

Scatch's hard work paid off-at least in his mind. He had a steady source of income looting people and fencing what he'd "repossessed" and he was no longer complete dead meat in a fight.  Scratch slowly began to believe he could do anything. He started fights he had no right starting-and no chance winning. He started stealing right in front of people's eyes. Long gone were the days of stealing only food and small, but valueable items. Scratch had started looting weapons and armour stores during business hours. 

Scratch thought he had cracked the code to thriving in the unforgiving wasteland of Kenshi. There had been close calls sure but he always made it out somehow.  With time however, Scratch had gotten bored with his routine and wanted more of an adventure, more of a thrill.  

For a while, Scratch stopped thieving and simply explored the world, moving from town to town.  He learned much on his travel-not just which places looked like promising marks.  

On these travels, Scratch had proven to be rather skillful swordsman, further elevating his ego and arrogance. He'd certainly come a long way from just flailing his sword around hoping it would cut his opponent. 

One day, Scratch stumbled upon a place called the Great Fortress, a once great clifftop fortress  of the Shek kingdom that had crumbled to no more than a walled ruin housing some soldiers in its only two buildings that had remained standing: the tower and the barracks. Scratch would soon become very well acquainted with these two buildings. Scratch walked through a heavily guarded gate and entered the fortress, not knowing that this gate was the only way in or out of the Great Fortress. Not knowing his life was about to change forever. 

Unfortunately,  the fortress did not have many valuables to take. He had wasted his time. Scratch, annoyed at being left empty handed decided to kill two birds with one stone. He was going to kidnap one or more of the Shek guards, run their pockets, strip them of their gear and leave. In doing so he would  not only make a decent amount of money but he would also be playing a hilarious (to him) prank on the Shek guards in return for their brothers in arms in Squinn always being a thorn in his side. 

He got caught. Unsurprisingly,  Scratch was arrested and imprisoned for his actions and he had accrued a bounty of around 500 Cats (which is the currency used in Kenshi). Scratch's sentence wasn't long at all but he was impatient, bored and restless. 

Enough was enough and Scratch started attempting escape, improving his lockpicking and stealth skill with each attempt. Yet he would get caught each time. At the time he did not know that his bounty was increasing with each arrest nor did he know that he had made a mistake entering the fortress. 

At this point the guards were getting fed up with his constantly getting of his cage and started attacking him on sight if they spotted him trying to escape, not to kill him but to incapacitate him for easier arresting. 
For days on end he was stuck in a loop. Get captured, escape, repeat. Get captured, escape retreat. Get captured escape repeat. Things wouldn't go so well the next time. 

During an escape escape, Scratch lost his leg, naturally making each escape attempt harder than what they used to be before. He was forced to crawl at a blistering speed of 1mph if he wanted to go anywhere. Not soon after, he lost his second leg.

Before he knew it he had been imprisoned for 30 in game days. But he never lost hope. All he had to do was crawl to the gate. His freedom would then be his.

Scratch had gotten quite lonely in his prison. For 30 days he had been the only prisoner. That was until a couple of bandits were caged next to him and a dead hiver was strung up right in front of him. He would die here if he didn't escape. Why? All of his escape attempts lead to him racking up a bounty of 70k cats.  A bounty this high would never expire and he would never be released. Even if he escaped, the bounty would remain for the rest of his life, unless he paid it off, which, naturally, he wasn't going to do. 

On one lucky day, the fortress was raid and Scratch used the opportunity to free himself and the other prisoners. These prisoners were his back up distraction, his last resort if things went south.


Luckily it was success. Scratch had managed to crawl past the guards throughout the fortress and those at the gate under the cover of night.

In hindsight, although seeming like the hardest time of his life, his imprisonment and constant escape attempts  were the easy part. 

The starving Scratch had to crawl for 2 weeks to reach his destination: a waystation where he could buy robotic limbs. It was painful, difficult and nerve wracking but eventually Scatch made it to his destination on the brink of death.

Scratch "borrowed" some legs, showing he'd not learnt his lesson. He made sure to get the fastest legs he could. He vowed to never get caught again and to never be forced be that slow ever again. He vowed to never be that weak ever again.

He trained and trained and trained. He became a master swordsman and a hawk eyed crossbowman-he did lose his arm in the process though. His bounty grew even higher and before long he became the most wanted man in the land. The legend of Scratch had been born 

Obviously, that's just the gist of what happened. Many of his later adventures were omitted too. Kenshi is a phenomenal game. Everything from its world, to its lore, to its style, to its music and much, much, much more. 

Not everyone will enjoy Kenshi and that's OK. But if you do enjoy it you will slowly but surely get addicted to it. 

If you can tolerate unforgiving games in which you're a nobody fighting to survive and make something of your life, play this game. 

If you enjoy games where you start from zero and earn your power , play this game. 

If you enjoy depressing yet somehow hopeful atmospheres, play this game. 

If you enjoy games where the lore is told through items and environmental storytelling, play this game.

 If you enjoy games that utilize scale to sell the insignificance of the player character, play this game.

If you like robots and think rust looks cool,  play this game.

Just try this game and give it a chance. Start your Kenshi addiction today! Join the beep nation.
Just the best game. Like Ever. You can do pretty much anything except for marry/have families. It's atmospheric and has a super strong modding community (with many expansions on the base game).

My limited experience playing kenshi:

Start the game as a wanderer, get killed immediatly by a thief stabbing you in the stomach.

Then try as two slaves; one loses a leg, the other dies. Get stuck in a loop where guards pick you up and put you down, making it impossible to do anything.

Play as a one armed beep in the desert. Progress much further than before. Get a cybernetic arm, proper armour, and get my skills high. End up dying to the religious fanatics when travelling across the map.

Tray playing as two slaves again; one beep and one shek. Beep loses his leg but doesn't get stuck in the same loop as previous slave run. Beep gets lockpicking skill of 70. Shek gets stronger and tougher. Plot to escape is forming...

An incredible work of art, straight from the heart and soul of its creators. This piece perfectly embodies the spirit of Oscar Wilde’s following quote, “The work of art is to dominate the spectator: the spectator is not to dominate the work of art.” When one plays Kenshi, the world comes to be their world. We are the blip in this world’s history, we are the starving armless drifter in the sands of the United Cities. Brilliant game, and an even more brilliant example of human individualism.
This game is great, sure it crashes every once in a while but what do you expect, its just such a charming enjoyable experience.

The game had lots of potential, I really wanted to enjoy it.  The devs wanted this to be a difficult game and the world to feel brutal ... but they have massively overdone it.  Imagine getting attacked every 4 min by a nearly impossible raid that takes 2 hours to recover over and over again.  Theres tons of mods, but none can help you, I believe the devs made anything that can help with combat unmoddable.  Rimworld is a very easy game compared to this.

So much to say but not a huge fan of writing reviews. The game is quite enthralling and i enjoy the challenge and randomness. Though i have played for some time, i still have yet to truly explore the map and all that it has to offer. 

My go to so far has to be the skeleton's and creating a outpost for trade and combat. Robotic ancient race. The early game is quite challenging, very doable. Don't be afraid to run.... very very far. Building is great, also, you can run away. Hide on the outskirts type thing and watch them run around looting. I usually leave a couple barrels full of food so they raid that. Robots don't need to eat, or sleep for that matter. 

Anyways, have fun and go wild. I plan on looking into exploring the ruins and building a combat squad. This game has been out for awhile, but i keep coming back. 

Looking forward to Kenshi 2 -

[h2] Kenshi is an unique and wonderfully weird game [/h2]

The learning curve is steep, but once you learn how to play, you'll be playing this for hours and hours. I've loved survival games for a long time, but after playing kenshi i sometimes have a hard time playing new ones because kenshi has everything i would want in that kind of game:

Complete freedom, one or many characters to manage and level up, a huge brutal world with lore as good as the fallout universe(if not better), many different fighting styles and much more. You can build a base and farm crops, you can be a mercenary, you can get enslaved or just become a miner - maybe even set up a place in a city where you craft clothing to sell and get rich? OR just smuggle in a bunch of drugs into the cities where it's illegal lol.

The lore is wonderful and interesting. Multiple factions to ally with, all morally awful in their own ways, while still having some small amount of positives. You'll literally get lied to and scammed and it's really hard to connect the dots. I love it. I thought i knew what was going on and then i read some lore theories and was really surprised.

My first and only tip is, once you get beat up, don't load. Roll with the punches. Only load if a character you love dies or something. It makes everything SO much more interesting and gives you an intense personal story to follow. 

The only bad things about Kenshi is how hard it is to learn, the dated graphics (which are oddly beautiful) and the fact that the game takes ages to load. You end up kinda ignoring this though, because this game was worked on for YEARS by a single person with so much passion.

[b] Kenshi is one of those games i return to once a year, and i can't wait for what Lo-Fi Games create next. [/b]

started off wanting to do a chill RPG party of misfits type run, ended off destroying several empires, committing multiple counts of terrorism, and skinning people alive so that they would fight better

it good :)

for the first 50 days, we were eaten by fogmen over and over and had to keep reloading saves. soon enough we became strong enough to leave the foglands. after months of training, we became strong. we recruited more soldiers, trained them, freed slaves, took down cities. We fed tengu to those same fogmen. Then the fog creature ate us all and I realized I hadn't saved the game in 10 hours. 10/10 would get eaten by the creature again.

Ok, nearly 10 years since release. When Kenshi 2? 

No complaints, much suffering? Good game, much self-roleplaying and self-creating story aspects? Good game, die on day one? Good game.
This is the best game I ever played. Getting eaten alive, becoming a slave, creating a human skin factory, etc. If you want a unique game. This is the one.

I would recommend new players to: turn off raids or you will be stomped, get a mod for increased xp (or something like stealth or strength legit took me like 10 hours just to get to ~40), and give yourself the ability to craft at least heart protectors so you can make something as far as armor goes. If you really want to build and not grind like mad, get the mod to spawn in build materials and iron plates so you can build away without it taking FOREVER. The weapon smithing is pretty much fine otherwise. Buff gate health, nuff said.


I am glad I finally played this game. It is likely worth your time. The game has a lot of jank for sure but it is really does well the 0 to hero idea. Your guy starts as complete crap, hardly anything is yours and even the most basic looter with own you one on one (if you ever could find them 1v1) even at moderate skill level. This game is really all about doing anything and everything to grind up your char from walking around overencumbered to sneaking everywhere to knocking out people in cages. Once you do get there, there is an amazing world out there full of cool stuff and massive. There are problems though: 
My biggest issue are the raids. Sometimes the game will send a group of like 10 - 40 guys at your base where you may have 3 or 4 guys who could only hope to take maybe 2 down if they're lucky, god forbid they send the black dragon ninjas at me when I am still working with cloth armor and basic metal weapons. If you're lucky, the best thing you can do is leave and let them destroy as walls and gates dont mean ♥♥♥♥. It is so crazy how 3 guys with the weakest weapons in the entire game can break down my giant metal gate in about 30 seconds. It makes walls feel pointless. I also have turrets but of course there is a skill for that so that didnt do anything. If youre new turn them off or avoid building anything as it wont last. Best case scenario, they come in and steal your food and leave, worst? they steal your guys and kill you.
The other issue I have is with armor. The basic idea to the game is grinding your skills and making a base, crafting, research (Rimworld like stuff really) and making your own armor and weapons. Why does the most basic armor ( other than I guess the leather armor but that's not the easiest to get depending on where you are) require a skill book that you find in essentially end game dungeons? I guess I could go and buy armor for all my guys taking lots of time and money but that's not as fun as making basic starter armor and getting at it. Or having to move somewhere to grow the cotton or plants to make the only armor I know. Same with helms and I think gloves, shoes, if u want to craft any youll have to be lucky and find a blueprint on someone otherwise you cannot make even the most basic armor for those slots.

I've never played anything like this. As others have said, you'll love it or hate it. I couldn't begin to describe this game. For years I passed on it because I thought it looked bad on the surface... but I was VERY WRONG. Game is art. Painful art. Very addictable. Steep learning curve. Unique.

Honestly Kenshi has been one of the best RPG experiences I've had in my life, and I've only down three playthroughs. My first one ended to a bonedog within the first five minutes whilst staying at the hub. Now I smuggle swamp weed while fighting off cannibal bugmen for spare change. 10/10 game will be continuing to play it and can't wait for the sequel!
I got enslaved and became crippled, great game

amazing game. full open world, free to roam around, no quests markers, you just walk around and have stuff happen to you and you learn as they happen. probably only true Real Life simulator in terms of how problems come at you and how you solve them.

Recommended for anyone who likes to "find the fun" in a game for lack of a better term, or for those who like a slower pace and grinding in a game.

I do recommend to take advantage of Steam's 2 hour Refund in case its not for you.  The downside is I feel it takes more than that for this game to get its hooks into you.

And since every play style is different here is my experience:

I started off as an average Joe, avoiding combat as much as possible, exploring a bit.  I saw there was a construction menu so I though I'd build a base and just treat it like Stardew Valley or a home in Skyrim.  

Nope, the walls you start with can't hold back anything.  So every few in-game days I just let raiders run around my base grabbing nothing because all I've been doing is mining and selling in the Bars nearby.  I raised my combat but luring a single bandit to a town and let the guards do the rest, getting in a few hits until i could take a single bandit out on my own.

I bought a second person for 6,000 and assigned us jobs to mine more, the idea being to save up to learn better walls.  I am just about to grow my first crop.

Anyways while all this sounds frustrating I enjoy it, just these small victories is enough of endorphins to keep me going.

Wonderful, amazing game that keeps me coming back.
Its not a terribly optimized game - but you can play with the graphics turned down enough if you are on a lower end PC. Especially certian areas of the game (swamp, im looking at you).
I recently heard they were developing Kenshi 2 on a new Unreal engine - good for them! Cook as long as you need to king!
A true masterpiece of the sandbox, RPG genre. The most freedom to do and be anything in a game I hvae ever seen. Nothing elsecomes close. A must buy
its genuinely a fun game, it has a learning curve but after you get past that its really fun

Thoroughly enjoying it. First two starts I died within 30 mins, on my 3rd go and figured out that hiring a band of mercs is the best way for me to survive early on (2000 caps a day)

Game's fun but most of it consists of walking through desolate emptiness and avoiding roving bands of enemies just to arrive at a village that is virtually the same as the last (with some exceptions).

I wish there was more stuff to find in between villages
I wish building stuff was easier; abandoned villages for instance, you can't rebuild -- can't even put a bed roll down

Looking forward to Kenshi 2, I read they stopped development on this one to focus on that one

Loved this game. I have played it multiple times over the years and every time I come back to the same feeling about it. Kenshi is next level gaming. Visuals definitely need improvement but content-wise, it is great. The other feedback is if most of legitimate mods would have been part of the main game, it would definitely make the lives of new gamers much easier.

This game is for very specific kinds of people who play games, people who are okay with making their own goals and using their own imagination to assign meaning to those goals, most importantly; people who are okay with playing a game that arguably hates them. This game will not hold your hand or even tell you what to do beyond some very light tutorial messages. If you're the kind of person who wants the game to provide actual quests or traditional story lines then this game isn't for you. There isn't really a set story line for you or your party. The "story" of the world is discovered through dialogue, rare books, and just generally paying close attention as you play. However, that story isn't about you or your party either, like I said this is the story of the world you play in. YOUR story is whatever you make it, and whatever your imagination can picture it as. 

I'd say if you play the first two hours before you can refund and you have trouble coming up with what to do or can't see yourself spending 10-100 hours on your playthrough the game might not be for you. If it DOES seem like an enjoyable game I thoroughly recommend installing some mods that improve performance (mostly loading times) and lessens some of the jank in the game, mods like re_Kenshi for example. Although, the base game is definitely stable enough to play normally.
Among the best of games I have ever played. That includes Bioshock and New Vegas. Absolutely hyped for the sequel. Thank you for this amazing experience.

From absolutely nothing scum to seasoned warrior to destroying a whole religious nation because they didn't like my bug guy to becoming a multimillionaire. easily one of my favorite games of all time i highly recommend it.

I love Kenshi. Likely even more than this game deserves. It's one my favourites games I've ever played.

Kenshi is rough and janky. I've noticed "Reset Squad Positions" emergency checkbox for loading a save. Guess what? I was already forced to use it twice. But the game usually doesn't break, doesn't stutter, just functions in it's own odd, slightly grotesque way. But I don't really care.

Kenshi is empty and unfinished, map is simply speaking too big for its content. You spend A LOT of time in fast forward mode watching your squad wander through wastelands. Don't get me wrong, this game offers plenty of possibilities and points of interests, but it really looks like something shipped because is good enough and already waaay to much work, and not because it's done. But hey, as I wrote, it's for sure sufficient, even if variety isn't that great and endgame... what's even and endgame in Kenshi run?

This game has no goal, it's just a roleplay sandbox for your people, but fills this role extremally well. Every core element needed for this is at least good.
For example, fighting is great, even if silly from many reasons, like how 1vsX works, how unarmed works, or how you can you micromanage your way to victory over vastly superior foes. You can chop off limbs of enemies, you can lose your own limbs and default state after a battle is "beaten, but not dead".
Another core element: building bases works also great, and world reacts to your new settlement.
Instead of going one by one, I will just highlight the main selling point of Kenshi:

Player agency. Game tries its best to react to your actions and tries to simulate in a simple, but effective way living world. It is far from perfect, but works definitively good enough to sell you an illusion, illusion needed for successful roleplay. As soon as you run out of interesting interactions to check, game loses its fuel, further playing/exploration feels pointless, since there is not plot or a goal here. Check my hours from this review though, it isn't coming anytime soon though.

Like Skyrim, naked game is problematic and it's best played with at least some basic mods, but you need to be careful, since some old mods are outdated, trying to fix already fixed issues.

9+/10.
Perfect game for me to sink my teeth into until Vintage Story gets some more juicy updates

my computer isn't that great but this game runs quite well. when i got the game i was skeptical at first but its pretty interesting keeps me occupied for hours especially if you add mods from the steam workshop i rate it a good 9.5/10
The less boring you are the less boring this game is. Play this if you like sandbox games, settlement management and RPG systems. Ball and gun gamers beware.
The producer is very capable. The game is great. It really has that 90s CRPG feel.
kinda high learning curve, but once u overcome it, kenshi is a great game with limitless replay value #cyber-beep

Its amazing, a true adventure, its unique, it deserves all of its praise. It's shameful that so many stupid ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ boring games get popular whilst this game has less popularity. I really like Kenshi.
Idk what runs worse the game or crumblejon 10/10 would recommend
Amazingly in depth game, cruel but when you start to understand the mechanics its really rewarding. Try not to save scum, as the fun is in your characters story unfolding regardless of what happens.

This is my favorite game that i cannot play. I know i have only put 9 hours into this game but about 8 of those hours were in one sitting and until 6 am. it is so addicting and hard to put down. 100% would recommend if you have better self control then i.

Crazy game. Huge respect to the developer.

It's hard to put into words why it's fun. Apart from the effort they've put into the huge handcrafted world and the charming combat system, it's just.. nah idk, this game deserves the glaze even though it's buggy and imperfect. 

It's kinda insane that it even works? Like a car model made out of wood. But this thing is not just fascinating, it's fun!! Probably because of the exploration and adventure. You need such a big d to just throw in the player into your world and have enough packed with just gameplay systems to keep them entertained, captivated. Whenever I encounter a bug or a limitation it just reminds me that projects like this would not exist without some madness, you know? 

Game development is not for everyone, man. We just won't see much of "this" in our lifetimes gg

Kenshi is crazy compelling. The progression is from nothing to godlike. Until you meet the next challenge and then you're humbled again. Base building and automation add another level to the sandbox. If only someone could meld Valheim and Kenshi I would have my perfect game.
hard to start but so worth it, would be cool if there were more fast forwarding options tho

I am weirdly obsessed with this game. It looks very retro, in a cool Fallout 1 kind of way. But the gameplay, is special. It is a world you want to explore. It's lore is hidden in books, ruins and half heard conversations in bars. It is also brutal. Survival is your victory condition.

I built a nice little shack near an iron deposit. Easy money. Money means food. If the religious zealots were not bad enough my food has become the object of desire for no fewer than three bandit gangs. I barely get enough peace to make the coin to hire mercenaries. So I put everything I could carry in my pack, sold the rest and hit the road. 

If I can make some coin elsewhere I will return in force. While that journey is only just beginning it has already become an adventure.
Fun, but I must just be bad at it. Couldn't find any viable source of income, so I didn't last long at all no matter what I tried, but it was a decent time-killer.
I just be mining copper for cats which is this games currency but overall great game I love it so far recruiting people are so fun to add more people to your team.

This game is something that it’s difficult to explain by words. Initially it can throw you off because graphics, very hard game start with zero purpose of play. I was charmed by reviews but after 4h I still did not see any sense of this game. I beg you give it a try for minimum few days and you will experience something that no other game has. I am playing on steam deck with 8 WAT and I cannot imagine better game. You can spend there 300h and I am sure that you still have seen only fragment of the game. After a week graphic doesn’t matter anymore but honestly I enjoy fight animations. In my opinion this game deserve recognition, please give it to try for longer than few days.
Nah, this game is lit. I have never had fun losing 3 round in a game and figuring things out without tutorials.

This game a cross between a more old school crpg/arpg and a colony management games. It's like a cross between the older fallouts or wasteland games with something like a rimworld like. This does mean that it does neither as well, but still does them well enough that the gameplay is serviceably enjoyable and fairly unique in a world that seems to have a lot to offer. 

The problem is there are so many obstacles to engaging with any of this that the enjoyment drops off quickly, first and foremost being the bugs, this game is nearly ten years old at this point and things like pathing and ai are barely functioning and many other parts of the game are in a similar state. Scripted events don't fire, people clip through walls, automated jobs break. Where my save is at now whenever I load all my squad fall through the wall they're on and get stuck. Given the intended high difficulty of the game, the biggest enemy just being the game itself has really undermined any desire to throw anymore time into this.

While I wouldn't usually complain about difficulty, the shear difficulty curve is another mark against it unfortunately. On the face of this is intentional, you are a person in a hostile world and trying to survive, but the game makes a big deal of this being a living word in which everyone has to deal with this hostility and this just isn't the case, which again makes the whole experience fall flat. You have to deal with every facet of survival while the game just spawns wave after wave of enemies who aren't behaving like food or injuries are critical concerns, which really undermines what seems to be the ethos of the game.

I get what people like about this game but not why it's such a massively positive rating, I don't dislike this game necessarily, there is a lot of good, interesting and unique elements that are engaging but they're behind a series of bugs and poor balancing that is almost certainly not going to be fixed at this point. Fine at a discount but I would feel really out of pocket if I paid full price for this.

This game has the ability to bring a revolutionary change to our definition of survival games. There being no tutorial giving the player a chance to learn on the go, it's also a very humbling game allowing the player to learn from their mistakes and adapt. I love this game and it is currently my #1!!! Keep up the good work Lo - Fi and you will do great things.
Its a great game, it just takes a bit more time than most people are used to. Its unrelenting and unbothered by your inexperience. Its awesome!

This game is really fun and addictive. The RPG and leveling your stats and building your own world is really fun. Its pretty hardcore and there are 'benefits' or at least features to most of the destruction that will happen to you. Choices have pretty big consequences.

Its a bit lacking on the resources to farm, pretty much just grinding iron/copper/stone the whole time. But the crafting and farming systems I guess are technically like higher tiered resources.

The only actual downside I have to mention (other then getting stomped constantly) is that the game is slightly buggy. The most aggravating part is the Jobs system, my guys are constantly being dumb, running into random areas, getting stuck, not understanding how to grab nearby resources (its confusing too because learning how to setup the automation is already not guided, but when the AI is not cooperating it escalates the frustration quickly). And if you have characters in different zones it takes a while to load everything again. Good thing is you can mostly just save and reload and it almost always fixes it. Sometimes I think it would be better if I only had to play one character because it can be very time consuming to manually get everyone on board with the program you want to get going.

But I still love the game, its made me stop playing everything else for now. Really hope a second one comes out soon. The depth and 'hidden' systems I've watched videos on are really intense. I have a hard enough time with bandits and can't wait to see the other 'horrors' in the game.

Listen to everyone when they say a moment comes that everything "clicks".  Go ahead and get your ass beat when you're first starting off.  Go ahead and let a character get captured by slavers.  Its all part of the process, and it all leads to something new.

This game is fun as ♥♥♥♥.

Great game for various genre fans. Base building, survival, RTS combat, numbie-go-up, emergent story telling. Not for everyone, but the vibe is real. Surprising amount of lore as well. Excited to see what the second game has in store.

I started playing a game with hunger set to easiest setting, got the feel for the  mechanics and some strategy ideas for survival, and then started a game "proper". It is really fun. One can't help but be a thief/assassin IMO, and use save-scumming to avoid accountability - I tell myself, "my guy is from the multiverse where he didn't fail that action" LMAO. Anyway, once stealth is high enough you can motor around while sneaking, being in people's line-of-sight but still not seeing you (in towns), utilizing PAUSE to loot because otherwise night comes too quickly while you're messing around reading item descriptions and looking at stats. I am yet to build an outpost to completion LOL, those damn bandits are everybloodywhere and if it's not them it's someone else. Fun to mess with them though for a solo elimination guerilla style, or luring them to city guards or monsters - so brutal watching parties get wiped. And when I have to do something grindy like science stuff, I'll watch an anime episode or two while time passes; it's a perfect break, then it's back for more: Exploring, Looting, KO'ing, Looting, Lockpicking, Looting, Luring to deaths, Looting - you get the idea.

Kenshi is not a game. It is a long-term agreement between you and suffering.

I started my journey with ambition. Within minutes I was chased, within hours I was unconscious, and by the end of the day I had developed a deep philosophical relationship with the word “help.”

There is no tutorial that truly prepares you for the experience of being mildly insulted by the world itself. Everything is stronger than you. Everything is faster than you. Even the rocks feel like they have opinions.

And yet… you keep going. Not because the game pushes you forward, but because quitting would feel like letting the desert win an argument.

Somehow, in between starvation, bandits, and questionable life choices, you start to understand the appeal. Progress is not measured in victory, but in how long you can stay upright before reality reminds you otherwise.

Every small success feels earned. Every failure feels educational. Every trip to the ground feels like the game asking: “Are you sure about this?”

Apparently I am. Repeatedly.

You can choose to start the game with riches and a hundred followers. Its fully modular to how you want to experience it.

What base kenshi offers is great  you do not need to change anything for the experience.

There is a charm to starting in a world that you will not be remembered. You are worse off than the ravenous animals who solely run across the plains to eat.

your decision making, careful planning, and ability to bounce back, is what will allow you to stay alive. 
Eat literal dust sandwiches, steal from dying bandits who had just been mauled by giant carnivores.
 Wear the blood soaked clothes they have and possibly need to fight off the now limbless survivors. Do you live in the hell of constant cannibal attacks? Do you choose to be under the oppressive and safe thumb of the holy nation?
You do not have freedom, you have the will to live and whatever it takes to stay off the menu.
